evidence: Ability resonates most with semantic slots tied to possibility's conceptual nature (ontological.semantic), its capacity to enable change (symmetry.transformative, symmetry.generative), and its practical utility (value_generation.functional). It also connects weakly to relational dynamics (ontological.relational) and contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent). Scores avoid overreach by focusing on core aspects of 'possible' without conflating with its symmetric pair (impossible).

The word 'able' strongly resonates with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic) as it defines the essence of possibility. It aligns with functional utility (value_generation.functional) and existential/essential resonance (resonance.essential/existential) due to its foundational role in defining what can exist or occur. Contingency (lawfulness.contingent) reflects its dependence on conditions, while transformative/generative symmetry (symmetry.transformative/generative) ties to enabling change. Lower scores reflect weaker or indirect connections.

The highest scores reflect the word's core meaning (ontological.semantic), practical utility (functional), and its role in creating new possibilities (symmetry.generative). It strongly ties to individual agency (scale.individual) and the experiential aspect of achieving goals (experience.creation).

Achievable strongly resonates with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), practical utility (functional value), and the understanding/experience needed to realize possibilities (epistemological.understanding, experience.comprehension). It also connects to generative symmetry (creating new possibilities) and contingent lawfulness (dependent on conditions).

Affirmable strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (4) as it relates to the meaning of possibility. It scores high in symmetry.generative (3) for creating new possibilities and lawfulness.contingent (3) as possibility depends on conditions. Scores 2-3 in epistemological slots (identification, understanding) and interconnection.catalytic (3) reflect its role in naming and triggering possibility.

The highest scores reflect 'assertable' as a semantic concept tied to possibility (ontological.semantic 3), its transformative potential (symmetry.transformative 3), and practical utility (value_generation.functional 4). Lower scores acknowledge its connection to identification/understanding (epistemological) and contingent conditions (lawfulness).

Attainable strongly resonates with semantic layers (ontological.semantic) and core essence (resonance.essential), as it directly concerns meaning and possibility's foundational nature. It ties to individual scale (scale.individual) and independent interconnection, emphasizing self-contained possibility. Predictable lawfulness (lawfulness.predictable) reflects expectation of feasibility under conditions, while functional value underscores practical utility.

The bathroom's physical existence (ontological.material:10) and functional utility (value_generation.functional:10) strongly resonate. Its scale (individual/community) and predictable construction (lawfulness.predictable:4) reflect common scenarios. Lower scores reflect contextual dependencies (e.g., contingent:2) or abstract connections (e.g., epistemological.identification:4).

Beer can resonates with possibility through its material existence (ontological.material 10), individual/societal scale use (scale.individual/society), predictable availability (lawfulness.predictable), functional utility (value.functional), and independent existence (interconnection.independent).

The caddy's physical presence (ontological.material) directly enables possibilities (functional value). Its role as a tool (catalytic interconnection) and dependency on its existence (contingent lawfulness) create tangible, surface-level links to possibility. The transformative symmetry reflects how caddies enable new actions, while aesthetic value acknowledges their design utility.

The verb 'caddy' (interpreted as enabling or assisting) resonates most with catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic, symmetry.transformative), relational dynamics (ontological.relational), and practical utility (value_generation.functional). It weakly connects to enabling possibilities through contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent) and creating new outcomes (experience.creation).

The word 'can' as possibility strongly resonates with semantic meaning (7) as it defines value and essence. It catalyzes change (5) by enabling actions and is generative (5) in creating new possibilities. Functional utility (6) reflects its practical role, while contingent lawfulness (5) highlights dependency on conditions.

The highest scores reflect 'can's core semantic meaning (5 in ontological.semantic), its role in generating new possibilities (5 in symmetry.generative), and its essential resonance with possibility's essence (5 in resonance.essential). Lower scores align with its influence over time (3), individual/societal scale (3/2), and contingent nature (4).

The word 'cannikin' (a small cup) strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as a physical object and value_generation.functional (10) due to its utility. Scores like scale.individual (2) and ontological.semantic (3) reflect its limited practical and symbolic roles in contexts where possibility might hinge on tangible items. Other scores are minimal, tied to rare narrative uses (e.g., permanence in historical texts).

The physical nature of 'closet' (ontological.material) directly ties to the possibility of storage. Its structural design (ontological.structural) and individual use (scale.individual) enable functional value (value_generation.functional). The cyclical opening/closing (symmetry.cyclical) and predictable utility (lawfulness.predictable) further align with PRP.possible.

The 'coffee can' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material) and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It suggests possibility through its physical presence (scale.individual, symmetry.generative) and routine use (symmetry.cyclical, lawfulness.predictable). Indication of possibility (temporal.indication) and contingent dependency (lawfulness.contingent) also score moderately.

The word 'come-at-able' strongly resonates with possibility's dependence on conditions (lawfulness.contingent), its generative potential (symmetry.generative), and its core essence (resonance.essential). It also aligns with individual/community/society scales and epistemological identification/understanding, reflecting its role in naming and structuring what is achievable.

The highest scores reflect the physical nature (material 10), community-scale utility (community 8), and inclusive purpose (inclusive 6) of comfort stations. Their functional value (functional 10) and predictable implementation (predictable 6) align with PRP.possible's focus on feasibility.

The word 'contingent' directly aligns with lawfulness.contingent (10), as it defines possibility dependent on conditions. It resonates with interconnection.chained (5) due to its reliance on cause-effect dependencies. Symmetry.generative (4) reflects how contingent possibilities lead to new outcomes. Ontological.semantic (4) captures its meaning-based dependency on conditions. Lower scores reflect weaker but real connections to temporal persistence, structural resonance, and functional value.

Convenience strongly resonates with FUNCTIONAL value (10) as it directly enables possibility through practical utility. It aligns with INDIVIDUAL scale (8) because convenience is often experienced personally. PREDICTABLE (7) reflects how reliability underpins possibility. MODERATE scores in MATERIAL, INFORMATIONAL, and STRUCTURAL ontological layers show its role in enabling systems. SUPERFICIAL resonance (6) captures its surface-level role in facilitating actions without deeper existential impact.

Doable strongly resonates with practical utility (value.functional), feasibility at individual/community levels (scale.individual/community), and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic). It connects weakly to contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent), catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic), and superficial resonance (resonance.superficial).

Executable strongly ties to actionable potential (ontological.informational, ontological.semantic) and its ability to drive change (interconnection.catalytic, resonance.essential). It operates across individual/community scales and depends on conditions (lawfulness.contingent). Lower scores reflect its focus on practical possibility rather than existential or transcendent aspects.

Feasible strongly resonates with functional value (5), structural/essential resonance (3 each), and catalytic interconnection (3). It operates at individual/community/society scales (2) and ties to identification (3), lawful predictability/contingency (2), and comprehension/creation (2).

The word 'head' (as in 'head start') resonates most strongly with individual agency (scale.individual), enabling creation of possibilities (experience.creation), generative outcomes (symmetry.generative), and practical utility (value_generation.functional). Scores are low elsewhere due to limited direct connections to the atom's definition of possibility.

John as a proper noun (individual scale) directly identifies a single entity (identification, material). His independence and potential role in enabling possibility (catalytic, contingent) score moderately. Symmetry.generative and functional/ethical value align with his agency in creating or enabling outcomes. Scores remain low elsewhere due to the abstract nature of 'possibility' and lack of explicit temporal or systemic context.

The highest scores reflect 'lav' as a concept tied to the semantic layer of possibility (5), its essential resonance with core notions of 'can be' (5), and its relational/structural underpinnings (2). Scores drop where 'lav' doesn't inherently imply scale, time, or value beyond abstract possibility.

Lavatory's physical nature (material:10) and functional utility (functional:10) directly tie to possibility. Its existence depends on conditions (contingent:3), and superficial resonance (2) reflects basic considerations. Independence (2) and inclusivity (2) relate to standalone availability and accessibility. Creation (2) addresses building it.

The word 'loo' (a physical toilet) strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it is a tangible object. Its functional utility aligns with value_generation.functional (10). Weak connections exist in epistemological slots (perception, identification, experience) due to direct sensory interaction and categorization. Scale.individual (2) reflects personal use, while lawfulness.predictable (2) notes its expected presence in structures. Other scores are 0 as they lack direct ties to the atom's 'possibility' context.

Manageable resonates most with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic 5), essential core (resonance.essential 5), and functional utility (value_generation.functional 5). It also ties to predictability (lawfulness.predictable 4) and relational/structural layers (ontological.relational/structural 3 each), reflecting its role in enabling possibility through controllable, stable systems.

Mathematical resonates strongly with structural patterns (ontological.structural 8), necessity (lawfulness.necessary 9), and functional utility (value_generation.functional 9). It also aligns with comprehension (experience.comprehension 7) and generative processes (symmetry.generative 5), reflecting its role in defining what is logically or systematically possible through abstract structures and universal principles.

The 'milk can' resonates most strongly with material existence (ontological.material:10) and its role in enabling practical utility (value_generation.functional:5). Temporal slots like emergence (3) and influence (3) reflect how the can's invention made milk preservation possible. Symmetry.generative (3) and lawfulness.predictable (4) highlight its enabling role in predictable storage systems.

The oilcan strongly resonates with its physical presence (ontological.material 10) and practical utility (value_generation.functional 10). Its routine use (lawfulness.predictable 5) and role in enabling machinery (interconnection.catalytic 2) reflect its functional role. Lower scores reflect its lack of deeper existential or transformative significance.

Possibility strongly resonates with transformation (when a possibility becomes reality), semantic meaning (its core essence), catalyzing change, and generative potential. It also aligns with emergent phenomena and the experience of creation (actualizing possibilities). Lower scores reflect its abstract nature and lack of direct material or cosmic scale.

The highest scores reflect 'possible' operating across individual/community/society scales (3-3-3), its semantic meaning (4), dependence on conditions (contingent:4), and its role in enabling transformation/generation (3 each). Lower scores align with its abstract nature and lack of direct ties to permanence, cosmic scales, or existential resonance.

The word 'possible' strongly resonates with meaning (ontological.semantic), core essence (resonance.essential), and generative processes (symmetry.generative). It is contingent on conditions (lawfulness.contingent) and connects to transformative dynamics (symmetry.transformative). Its understanding and identification are central (epistemological slots), and it operates at individual and cosmic scales.

Possibleness strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (4) as it defines meaning and value of possibility. It scores highly in catalytic interconnection (3) and symmetry.transformative (3), reflecting its role in triggering change. Lawfulness.contingent (3) underscores its dependence on conditions, while value_generation.functional (3) and experience.creation (3) highlight practical and creative applications.

Practicable strongly resonates with functional value (7) and contingent lawfulness (4), as it emphasizes feasibility tied to conditions. It connects moderately with understanding (4) and structural ontology (1), reflecting its role in grasping actionable possibilities within systems.

The strongest resonance is with ontological.material (10), as 'privy' denotes a physical structure. Weak links include its individual scale (1), perceptibility (1), functional utility (2), and minor ties to temporal establishment (1) and lawfulness.predictable (1). These reflect its physical presence and practical role in contexts where possibility might depend on material conditions.

The highest scores reflect 'public convenience' as a material, functional, and generative concept tied to ongoing societal needs. Its essential resonance stems from fulfilling core communal requirements, while its material aspect anchors it in physical infrastructure.

Public toilets resonate most with material existence (ontological.material) and functional utility (value_generation.functional). Their predictable presence in urban settings (lawfulness.predictable) and community-scale operation (scale.community) also score highly. Lower scores reflect minimal resonance with abstract concepts like existential meaning or ethical value.

Realizable strongly resonates with semantic slots tied to understanding (epistemological.understanding), structural patterns (ontological.semantic), and transformative potential (symmetry.transformative). It also connects to contingency (lawfulness.contingent) and creation (experience.creation), reflecting its role in making abstract possibilities concrete.

Restroom's resonance is strongest in its physical presence (ontological.material), predictability of human needs (lawfulness.predictable), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). Scores for perception/identification/experience reflect its tangible, everyday role. Scores above 0 are grounded in real-world contexts where 'restroom' relates to possibility (e.g., 'Is there a restroom available?').

The soda can resonates most strongly with material existence (ontological.material), individual scale, and functional value. Its presence is directly perceivable and predictable, aligning with PRP.possible's sense of 'able to be done.' Lower scores reflect weak ties to understanding its structure or comprehension of its possibilities.

The highest scores reflect its physical tangibility (material:10), practical utility (functional:9), clear identification as an object (identification:8), and predictable use (predictable:7). Lower scores relate to its role in individual/community contexts and generative function as a storage tool.

Tin's physical nature (ontological.material) strongly ties to its possibility of use. Its functional applications (value_generation.functional) and predictable properties (lawfulness.predictable) support this. Identification and understanding of tin enable recognizing its possibilities.

The verb 'tin' in the context of possibility weakly resonates with catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic), semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), essential core resonance (resonance.essential), generative symmetry (symmetry.generative), emergent unpredictability (lawfulness.emergent), discovery (experience.discovery), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). Scores are low due to the ambiguity of 'tin' as a verb in this context.

The word 'tin can' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it is a physical object, and value_generation.functional (10) due to its practical utility. It also scores moderately in scale.individual (5) as it is a single-use item, and lawfulness.predictable (5) since its use is expected and routine. These scores align with its tangible, everyday presence and reliability, fitting the 'possible' atom.

Toilet strongly resonates with FUNCTIONAL value (8) as a practical necessity, MATERIAL existence (8) as a physical object, and PREDICTABLE routine (6). SEMANTIC meaning (3) reflects its role as a basic human need. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to existential or systemic transformation.

Toilet facilities resonate most with practical utility (functional value), their material existence (ontological.material), and their role in enabling health and societal stability (scale.community/society, lawfulness.predictable). Scores reflect their tangible, enduring presence and foundational role in infrastructure, while avoiding over-scoring abstract or speculative connections.

Viable strongly resonates with structural ontology (5), essential resonance (5), and contingent lawfulness (5) because it depends on conditions and relates to foundational structures. It also aligns with functional value (5) as it implies practical feasibility. Lower scores reflect its focus on possibility without existential or cosmic implications.

W.C. (water closet) as a physical entity (ontological.material=10) enables practical functionality (value_generation.functional=10). Its presence catalyzes possibilities (interconnection.catalytic=3) and aligns with generative symmetry (symmetry.generative=3) as it makes certain actions feasible. Weak ties exist to perception (e.g., seeing a restroom), identification (naming it), and structural patterns (e.g., building design).

The word 'wash room' strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical existence) and value_generation.functional (practical utility) in the context of possibility. It weakly relates to lawfulness.contingent (dependence on conditions like space or regulations). Most other slots lack direct relevance to the possibility of a wash room's existence or use.

Washroom's physical existence (ontological.material) enables the possibility of use (functional value). Its presence in individual spaces (scale.individual) and predictable availability (lawfulness.predictable) directly tie to PRP.possible. Lower scores reflect minimal resonance beyond practical utility and structural enablement.

Water closet's strong resonance lies in its material existence (ontological.material=10) and functional utility (value_generation.functional=10), which directly tie to the possibility of practical actions. Its presence as an established, predictable fixture (temporal.establishment=2, lawfulness.predictable=3) supports the idea of possibility. Weak scores reflect tangential connections like its role in daily routines (symmetry.cyclical=2) or sensory perception (epistemological.perception=3).

Workable strongly resonates with functional utility (practicality), relational dependencies (interconnectedness), and semantic meaning (possibility's core essence). It also aligns with understanding (grasping feasibility), predictability (expected outcomes), and individual scale (applying to single tasks).
